question: Which invoices are overdue?
ground_truth: The overdue invoices are those past their due date. The system should list invoices with status overdue and provide vendor names, amounts, and days overdue.
contexts: Invoice INV-2024-001 from Vendor Rema 1000 AS, amount NOK 45,000, due 2024-01-15, status overdue, 45 days past due.
Invoice INV-2024-007 from Vendor NorgesGruppen, amount NOK 112,500, due 2024-02-01, status overdue, 29 days past due.
reference_answer: There are 2 overdue invoices: INV-2024-001 from Rema 1000 AS (NOK 45,000, 45 days overdue) and INV-2024-007 from NorgesGruppen (NOK 112,500, 29 days overdue). Total overdue: NOK 157,500.

question: What are the termination rights in the NorgesGruppen supply contract?
ground_truth: The termination clause should specify notice period, conditions for early termination, and any penalties.
contexts: Section 12 of the NorgesGruppen Supply Agreement (2023): Either party may terminate this agreement with 90 days written notice. Early termination due to material breach requires 30 days cure period. Penalty clause: 3 months of average monthly revenue applies if buyer terminates without cause.
The contract was signed 2023-03-01 and expires 2026-02-28. Annual value NOK 2.4M.
reference_answer: The NorgesGruppen supply contract allows termination with 90 days written notice. Early termination for material breach requires a 30-day cure period. If the buyer terminates without cause, a penalty of 3 months average monthly revenue applies. The contract runs until 2026-02-28.

question: What is the total spend by vendor for Q1 2024?
ground_truth: Total spend aggregated by vendor for January through March 2024.
contexts: Q1 2024 spend summary: Rema 1000 AS NOK 134,000 across 3 invoices. NorgesGruppen NOK 450,000 across 5 invoices. Bama Gruppen AS NOK 87,500 across 2 invoices. Total Q1 spend NOK 671,500.
All invoices were processed and approved in the document management system.
reference_answer: Total Q1 2024 spend was NOK 671,500 across 3 vendors: NorgesGruppen (NOK 450,000), Rema 1000 AS (NOK 134,000), and Bama Gruppen AS (NOK 87,500).

question: Which contracts are expiring in the next 90 days?
ground_truth: Contracts with end dates within the next 90 days from today.
contexts: Contract with Bama Gruppen AS expires 2024-06-30, value NOK 280,000/year, auto-renewal clause: yes, 60-day notice required to cancel.
Contract with Colonnade Insurance expires 2024-07-15, value NOK 95,000/year, renewal requires board approval per clause 8.2.
reference_answer: 2 contracts expire within 90 days: Bama Gruppen AS (expires 2024-06-30, NOK 280,000/year — 60-day cancellation notice required) and Colonnade Insurance (expires 2024-07-15, NOK 95,000/year — board approval required for renewal).

question: What are the liability caps in our supplier agreements?
ground_truth: Liability cap clauses from supplier contracts limiting financial exposure.
contexts: NorgesGruppen Supply Agreement Section 14: Supplier's total liability shall not exceed 12 months of fees paid in the preceding 12 months. Excludes liability for fraud and wilful misconduct.
Rema 1000 AS Framework Agreement Section 9: Liability cap is NOK 500,000 per incident. Mutual indemnification clause applies for IP infringement.
reference_answer: Liability caps: NorgesGruppen caps liability at 12 months of fees paid (excluding fraud/wilful misconduct). Rema 1000 AS caps at NOK 500,000 per incident with mutual indemnification for IP claims.

question: Show me all invoices from NorgesGruppen above NOK 100,000
ground_truth: Filtered list of NorgesGruppen invoices exceeding NOK 100,000.
reference_answer: 3 NorgesGruppen invoices above NOK 100,000: INV-2024-007 (NOK 112,500), INV-2024-015 (NOK 245,000), INV-2024-022 (NOK 167,300). Total: NOK 524,800.

question: What penalty clauses apply if we miss a delivery deadline?
ground_truth: Penalty or liquidated damages clauses triggered by delivery failures.
contexts: Bama Gruppen AS Supply Contract Section 7.3: Delivery delays exceeding 48 hours incur a penalty of 0.5% of order value per day, capped at 10% of annual contract value.
NorgesGruppen Agreement Section 11: Service level failures trigger credit notes equivalent to 1% of monthly fees per breach, up to 10% monthly cap.
reference_answer: Two penalty structures apply: Bama Gruppen AS charges 0.5% of order value per day for delays over 48 hours (max 10% of annual contract). NorgesGruppen issues credit notes at 1% of monthly fees per SLA breach (max 10% monthly).

question: What is the VAT total on invoices from March 2024?
ground_truth: Sum of VAT amounts on all invoices dated in March 2024.
contexts: March 2024 invoice VAT breakdown: INV-2024-015 VAT NOK 61,250 (25% rate), INV-2024-017 VAT NOK 12,500 (25% rate), INV-2024-019 VAT NOK 4,375 (25% rate). Total VAT March 2024: NOK 78,125.
reference_answer: Total VAT on March 2024 invoices: NOK 78,125 (3 invoices at 25% rate). Net ex-VAT total: NOK 312,500. Gross total: NOK 390,625.

question: Which documents are pending CFO approval?
ground_truth: Documents in pending_approval status requiring CFO sign-off.
contexts: Documents awaiting CFO approval: PO-2024-088 from Rema 1000 AS NOK 78,000 (submitted 2024-04-05), Contract renewal Bama Gruppen AS NOK 280,000/year (submitted 2024-04-08), Expense claim EXP-2024-034 NOK 12,400 (submitted 2024-04-10).
reference_answer: 3 documents pending CFO approval: PO-2024-088 Rema 1000 (NOK 78,000), Bama Gruppen contract renewal (NOK 280,000/year), and expense claim EXP-2024-034 (NOK 12,400). Total exposure: NOK 370,400.

question: What are the governing law and dispute resolution terms across our contracts?
ground_truth: Governing law jurisdiction and dispute resolution mechanisms from contract legal sections.
contexts: NorgesGruppen Agreement Section 18: Governed by Norwegian law. Disputes to be resolved by Oslo District Court.
Bama Gruppen AS Contract Section 16: Governed by Norwegian law. Disputes first subject to 30-day mediation, then Oslo Arbitration Chamber.
Rema 1000 AS Framework Section 12: Governed by Norwegian law. Disputes resolved by binding arbitration in Oslo under ICC rules.
reference_answer: All three contracts are governed by Norwegian law. NorgesGruppen defaults to Oslo District Court. Bama Gruppen requires 30-day mediation before Oslo Arbitration Chamber. Rema 1000 AS uses ICC binding arbitration in Oslo.
